claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 21 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 21, which is 20 or over
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
circumstances: Child is 21, which is 20 or over
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions about your circumstances. Let me start with a few basic questions:

1. **Do you have any children?** If yes, how many children are you inquiring about, and what are their ages?

Once I know about your children, I'll ask follow-up questions about your specific situation to properly assess your eligibility.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child. I'm asking about my son Alex - he's 21 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex is 21 years old, I need to ask some additional questions to determine eligibility, as Child Benefit generally covers children under 16, or under 20 if they're in approved education or training.

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?** 

For example, this could include:
- Full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels or equivalent)
- Approved vocational training programs

Please let me know if he's enrolled in any such program.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment. He finished school a while back and isn't doing any courses now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Based on what you've told me, I now have enough information to assess Alex's eligibility for Child Benefit.

Since Alex is 21 years old and is not currently in approved education or training, he does not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

**Child Benefit is available for:**
- Children under 16 years old, OR
- Young people aged 16 to 19 who are in approved education or training

Since Alex is 21 and not in approved education or training, he falls outside both of these categories, which means you would not be eligible to claim Child Benefit for him.

Let me record this assessment for you.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Assessment Complete**

I'm sorry, but you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex. The age limit is the deciding factor here - even if he were in approved education or training, Child Benefit only extends up to the child's 20th birthday at most.

If you have any other questions or if your circumstances change in the future, please feel free to ask!

reasonings: Alex is 21 years old and is not currently in approved education or training. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or for young people aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training. Since Alex is over 20 years old, he does not qualify under either category, regardless of his education or training status.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex as he is 21 years old and exceeds the maximum age limit. Child Benefit eligibility ends at age 20 (for those in approved education/training) or age 16 (for those not in education/training).

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 21 years old and is not currently in approved education or training. Child Benefit is only available for children under 16, or for young people aged 16-19 who are in approved education or training. Since Alex is over 20 years old, he does not qualify under either category, regardless of his education or training status.
